title: Incorrect Count of Set Bits in Binary Representation

statement.markdown:
Count the number of set bits in a given integer n.

You have been given a function `countSetBits` which takes an integer n as input and returns the count of set bits in the binary representation of n.

However, the function contains a subtle bug and does not produce the correct output for all possible inputs. Your task is to find and fix the bug in the function.
statement.outputFormat: The count of set bits in the binary representation of n
statement.notes: Time limit: 1 second. Space complexity should be O(1).

examples.explanation: The binary representation of 5 is 101, which has two set bits.
The binary representation of 10 is 1010, which has two set bits.

hints.text: Print out the binary representation of the input n to understand the problem.

editorial.title: Editorial title
editorial.optimalSolution: Count the number of 1's in the binary representation of the input n
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness for the optimal solution
